name: Web Systems Development

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: A coherent web spine exists with real depth (IS 601 Web Systems Development feeds IS 690 Web Services and Middleware, IS 634 Information Retrieval, and IS 631; IS 665 feeds IS 688 Web Mining), but the referenced pool balloons to 36 courses dominated by an unrelated data-science/AI suite (DS 669 Reinforcement Learning, DS 680 NLP, CS 677 Deep Learning) and doctoral research courses (DS 725/726, DS 792B, CS 726, DS 700B/701B thesis). This scope drift badly undermines coherence for a web-development certificate.
dimensions.suggestion: Prune to a focused web-development pathway (IS 601, IS 690, IS 634, IS 688, CS 602 plus a modern front-end/full-stack course) and remove the unrelated data-science, AI, and doctoral-research courses.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 2 with sensible web chains (IS 601 to IS 690/IS 634; IS 665 to IS 688), but missingCourseCount is 5 and the danglers (CS 280, MATH 333, DS 790, DS 790A, INTD 799) are undergraduate prerequisites and doctoral research courses that should not be part of a web certificate at all; the danglers are a symptom of the over-broad course inclusion.
dimensions.suggestion: Remove the doctoral research references (DS 790, DS 790A, INTD 799) and the undergraduate prerequisite codes so the certificate's graph reflects only its web-development courses.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes are present in the data, a real gap for a certificate whose graduates should be able to demonstrate specific web-development competencies.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish outcomes such as building web systems, developing web services/middleware, and applying information retrieval and web mining.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits and creditReconcileDelta are null (expected for a certificate), but with a 36-course referenced pool and no stated total or required count, the credential is both opaque and far too broad to read as a focused ~12-credit certificate.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credits (e.g., 12) and restrict the listing to a coherent four-course web-development pathway.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page mixes 36 courses spanning web development, the full data-science/AI curriculum, and doctoral thesis/research placeholders, with five dangling references and no overview or outcomes; the mismatch between the certificate name and its content is a real presentation defect.
dimensions.suggestion: Rewrite the page to list only web-systems courses with a short overview, and remove the data-science, AI, and doctoral entries that do not belong.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The genuinely web-focused courses lean dated: IS 601 (open-source web development), IS 690 (web services/middleware/SOA), IS 634 (information retrieval), and IS 688 (web mining) reflect a 2000s-era stack, while the modern web stack (JavaScript/TypeScript SPA frameworks, Node.js, REST/GraphQL APIs, cloud/serverless deployment, containers, and web security) is absent; the bundled ML/AI courses are current but off-topic.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a modern full-stack course (JS/TS frameworks, REST/GraphQL, cloud deployment) and a web-security course (OWASP Top 10, OAuth) to replace the SOA-era emphasis.

descriptionQuality.justification: Most sampled courses have present and specific descriptions (IS 690 middleware/interoperability; IS 634 crawling/indexing/vector-space IR; IS 688 web-hyperlink and usage mining), but several bundled research and independent-study entries are thin placeholders (DS 786 'Introduction to selected topics in data science'; DS 700B/701B/725/726 restriction-only stubs) that drag the aggregate down.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Many prerequisites are clear (IS 601 into IS 690/IS 634; DS 677 requires DS 675 or CS 675), but five references dangle, some prereq fields carry description prose (CS 602 and IS 601 'NONE Students will...'), and the out-of-scope courses muddy the graph's consistency.

missingTopics.rationale: IS 601 teaches open-source web development but no current client-side framework stack, the core of contemporary web development.
IS 690 covers SOA-era web services but not modern API-first, Node/REST/GraphQL development.
No course covers deploying web systems to the cloud, now a baseline professional skill.
Building web systems (IS 601) without an application-security course leaves a critical gap.

missingTopics.topic: Responsive, progressive, and accessible web apps
missingTopics.rationale: Modern front-end practice (responsive design, PWAs, accessibility) is absent from the web spine.

emergingTrends.rationale: Web systems increasingly deploy to serverless/edge platforms (Vercel, Cloudflare), which IS 601/IS 690 should incorporate.

emergingTrends.trend: AI-assisted and LLM-integrated web apps (RAG, chat UIs)
emergingTrends.rationale: IS 634 (information retrieval) and IS 688 (web mining) are natural bridges to retrieval-augmented, LLM-powered web applications.
Decoupled front-end/back-end architectures are now standard and should modernize IS 690's middleware focus.
Near-native browser performance via WebAssembly is expanding the web platform in ways the current courses ignore.
Interactive real-time web applications require event-driven patterns absent from the SOA-era curriculum.

topSuggestions: Prune the 36-course referenced pool to a coherent four-course web-development pathway and remove the unrelated data-science, AI, and doctoral entries.
Modernize the curriculum with a full-stack JS/TS plus cloud-deployment course and a web-security (OWASP) course.
State total credits and the required courses so the certificate reads as a focused credential.
Publish program learning outcomes describing the web-development competencies earned.
Remove the dangling doctoral and undergraduate course references so the prerequisite graph reflects only the certificate's courses.
